hints.text: Plate long white + standard EU blue strip left
Bollards **thin, slightly curved**: rectangular reflector front + two circular back, **angled black strip**, often numbers below (EE/LT differ)
Poletops with **hook-shaped insulators alternating left-right-left** (much rarer in the other Baltics)
Chevrons **white w/ red arrows** (like Lithuania/Poland; Estonia/Russia/Ukraine = opposite)
Blue km marker **parallel to the road**, number on one side only (Estonia perpendicular, Lithuania 45°)
Signs = **thick red border + thin white outline**; A-type guardrails w/ white or red reflectors
Pedestrian sign **5 stripes, noticeably long legs** — unique in the Baltics (Estonia & Lithuania = 3 stripes)
Bus-stop sign bus has **5 windows** (Estonia & Lithuania = 4)
**Latvian** (Baltic): macron vowels **Ā Ē Ī**, hook consonants **Ģ Ķ Ļ Ņ**; street = **iela**; tall-rectangle house numbers
Architecture wood + white brick w/ steep corrugated metal roofs; **Riga** downtown = art-nouveau (Vienna-like); red & black Gen4 cars
